title: agent/m3-secure-cloud-persistence
body:
## M3 Cloud Persistence Integration

This PR implements the requested secure, production-shaped cloud persistence while retaining full local-first capabilities.

### What Changed
*   **Database & RLS:** Added `20260710232400_create_gear_systems.sql` implementing the schema and strict RLS policies ensuring users can only read and modify their own records.
*   **Auth State Machine:** Implemented passwordless email OTP auth in `src/auth/useSupabaseAuth.ts` capturing states like `sending-otp`, `verifying-otp`, `signed-in`, etc.
*   **API & Optimistic Concurrency:** Abstracted standard CRUD logic into explicit typed actions (`list`, `create`, `update`, `rename`, `delete`) in `src/persistence/gear-systems.ts`, preventing overlapping saves through `updated_at` checks.
*   **Strict Typing:** Re-generated `database.types.ts` from local postgres instance and properly extracted core definitions using `gear-system-definition.ts` to strictly keep out transient derived state (mesh ratios, geometric logic).
*   **UI Integration:** Added `AuthPanel` and `SavedSystemsPanel` embedded into the workspace, adapting to the user's login state and handling custom vs guided model overrides safely.

### How to test locally
1. Ensure the Supabase CLI is running `npx supabase start`
2. Boot app `pnpm dev` and verify it operates normally without auth.
3. Use the Auth Panel to submit your email, fetch the OTP code locally using `npx supabase status` -> Inbucket URL.
4. Verify code. Once logged in, save the workspace, branch new copies, edit them, and delete them.
5. Notice all your work is retained and the local canvas adapts properly to sign-ins and sign-outs.
head: agent/m3-secure-cloud-persistence
